title: mentha sylvestris subsp. velutina
intro: Mentha sylvestris subsp. velutina, also known as woolly mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae and is native to Europe. It is a perennial herb with a strong, minty fragrance and medicinal properties that have been utilized for centuries. The plant's essential oils, which contain menthone and menthol, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as relieving digestive issues, reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting infections. Woolly mint can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to soothe the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
